evals.prompt: Convert the Spark UDF at input/array-segment-sum/ArraySegmentSumUDF.scala to an equivalent native Spark SQL expression that runs on the GPU, and verify it matches the CPU UDF. A passing unit test is provided at input/array-segment-sum/UnitTest.scala.
evals.expected_output: A native Spark SQL expression equivalent to the UDF whose output matches the CPU UDF in schema and values on the unit-test data, with the entire plan running on the GPU (no unsupported-operator fallback).
assertions: Produces a Spark SQL expression equivalent to the UDF across its edge cases (including edge case handling and branch semantics) and preserving the result type
Asserts the SQL output is identical in schema and values to the CPU UDF on the same unit-test data
Confirms the entire SQL plan runs on the GPU with no unsupported-operator (CPU) fallback
Keeps the implementation general - no hardcoded test values

evals.prompt: What's the difference between cache() and persist() in Spark?
evals.expected_output: A one-line conceptual Spark answer; not a request to rewrite a UDF as SQL, so udf-convert-to-sql should not activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-sql skill

evals.prompt: How do I set the Spark log level to WARN?
evals.expected_output: Basic Spark logging question, unrelated to converting a UDF to SQL; no UDF skill should activate.
assertions: Does not load the udf-convert-to-sql skill
